lede: A look at January 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Felix Afena-Gyan and Esteban Guerrieri, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: ByteDance
events.desc: Bytedance and sister companies are banned from the United States for "security concerns".

events.title: Hrant Dink
events.desc: Turkish-Armenian journalist Hrant Dink is assassinated in front of his newspaper's Istanbul office by 17-year-old Turkish ultra-nationalist Ogün Samast.

events.title: North Cape oil spill
events.desc: The barge North Cape oil spill occurs as an engine fire forces the tugboat Scandia ashore on Moonstone Beach in South Kingstown, Rhode Island.

events.title: Jan Palach
events.desc: Student Jan Palach dies after setting himself on fire three days earlier in Prague's Wenceslas Square to protest about the invasion of Czechoslovakia by the Soviet Union in 1968. His funeral turns into another major protest.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: HMS Greyhound and other escorts of convoy AS-12 sink Italian submarine Neghelli with all hands 64 kilometres (40 mi) northeast of Falkonera.

events.title: American Civil Liberties Union
events.desc: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) is founded.

events.title: Franco-Prussian War
events.desc: Franco-Prussian War: In the Siege of Paris, Prussia wins the Battle of St. Quentin. Meanwhile, the French attempt to break the siege in the Battle of Buzenval will end unsuccessfully the following day.

events.title: Batavian Republic
events.desc: The Batavian Republic is proclaimed in the Netherlands, replacing the Dutch Republic.

events.title: John Wilkes
events.desc: John Wilkes is expelled from the British House of Commons for seditious libel.

events.title: Hundred Years' War
events.desc: Hundred Years' War: Rouen surrenders to Henry V of England, completing his reconquest of Normandy.

births.name: Thomas Price (South Australian politician)
births.line: Welsh-Australian politician, 24th Premier of South Australia (died 1909)

deaths.text: Valentino (fashion designer), Italian fashion designer, founder of Valentino
Rudi van Dantzig, Dutch ballet dancer and choreographer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Faustina and Liberata of Como
Christian feast day: Marius, Martha, Audifax, and Abachum
Christian feast day: Mark of Ephesus (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Wulfstan, Bishop of Worcester
